humanSummary.text: Final audit found no P0/P1 blockers for ITOTORI-011. The remaining implementationHash provenance concern is tracked as a non-blocking P3 DAG follow-up.

findings.title: implementationHash remains declared metadata
findings.description: The registry can record implementationHash metadata, but the hash is trusted as declared metadata rather than derived from or verified against the deterministic tool implementation.

evidence.detail: Final wave 12 audit recorded implementationHash provenance as a possible P3 follow-up with no P0/P1 completion blocker.

findings.impact: Tool provenance can imply stronger implementation identity than the system has verified, which weakens later reproducibility and forensic checks.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Registry implementationHash values are derived from canonical implementation artifacts or verified against source or binary inputs before they are treated as implementation identity.
Hash mismatches fail with diagnostics that name the tool, declared hash, and verification source without trusting the declared metadata.
Externally supplied hashes that cannot be verified are labeled as declared metadata rather than verified implementation identity.
Deterministic tool provenance events distinguish verified implementation hashes from declared or unknown implementation hashes.

auditFocus: Trusted metadata mistaken for code identity
Hash mismatch diagnostics that trust the declared hash
Tool provenance events that hide unverifiable implementation hashes
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Tracked as planned DAG node ITOTORI-054 for wave 12 completion bookkeeping.
